function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.
Important: cloud sandboxes may be cleaned up, idled out, or recreated between turns. Persistent filesystem means files can resume later; it does NOT guarantee a continuously running machine or surviving background processes. Use terminal sandboxes for task work, not durable hosting.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

check_interval.description: Seconds between automatic status checks for background processes (gateway/messaging only, minimum 30). When set, I'll proactively report progress.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified when the process finishes — no polling needed. Use this for tasks that take a while (tests, builds, deployments) so you can keep working on other things in the meantime.

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to a file, completely replacing existing content. Use this instead of echo/cat heredoc in terminal. Creates parent directories automatically. OVERWRITES the entire file — use 'patch' for targeted edits.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

# AGENTS.md - Personal Knowledge Base Schema

> Adapted from [Andrej Karpathy's LLM Knowledge Base](https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f) architecture.
> Instead of ingesting external articles, this system compiles knowledge from your own AI conversations.

## The Compiler Analogy

```
daily/          = source code    (your conversations - the raw material)
LLM             = compiler       (extracts and organizes knowledge)
knowledge/      = executable     (structured, queryable knowledge base)
lint            = test suite     (health checks for consistency)
queries         = runtime        (using the knowledge)
```

You don't manually organize your knowledge. You have conversations, and the LLM handles the synthesis, cross-referencing, and maintenance.

---

## Architecture

### Layer 1: `daily/` - Conversation Logs (Immutable Source)

Daily logs capture what happened in your AI coding sessions. These are the "raw sources" - append-only, never edited after the fact.

```
daily/
├── 2026-04-01.md
├── 2026-04-02.md
├── ...
```

Each file follows this format:

```markdown
# Daily Log: YYYY-MM-DD

## Sessions

### Session (HH:MM) - Brief Title

**Context:** What the user was working on.

**Key Exchanges:**
- User asked about X, assistant explained Y
- Decided to use Z approach because...
- Discovered that W doesn't work when...

**Decisions Made:**
- Chose library X over Y because...
- Architecture: went with pattern Z

**Lessons Learned:**
- Always do X before Y to avoid...
- The gotcha with Z is that...

**Action Items:**
- [ ] Follow up on X
- [ ] Refactor Y when time permits
```

### Layer 2: `knowledge/` - Compiled Knowledge (LLM-Owned)

The LLM owns this directory entirely. Humans read it but rarely edit it directly.

```
knowledge/
├── index.md              # Master catalog - every article with one-line summary
├── log.md                # Append-only chronological build log
├── concepts/             # Atomic knowledge articles
├── connections/          # Cross-cutting insights linking 2+ concepts
└── qa/                   # Filed query answers (compounding knowledge)
```

### Layer 3: This File (AGENTS.md)

The schema that tells the LLM how to compile and maintain the knowledge base. This is the "compiler specification."

---

## Structural Files

### `knowledge/index.md` - Master Catalog

A table listing every knowledge article. This is the primary retrieval mechanism - the LLM reads this FIRST when answering any query, then selects relevant articles to read in full.

Format:

```markdown
# Knowledge Base Index

| Article | Summary | Compiled From | Updated |
|---------|---------|---------------|---------|
| [[concepts/supabase-auth]] | Row-level security patterns and JWT gotchas | daily/2026-04-02.md | 2026-04-02 |
| [[connections/auth-and-webhooks]] | Token verification patterns shared across Supabase auth and Stripe webhooks | daily/2026-04-02.md, daily/2026-04-04.md | 2026-04-04 |
```

### `knowledge/log.md` - Build Log

Append-only chronological record of every compile, query, and lint operation.

Format:

```markdown
# Build Log

## [2026-04-01T14:30:00] compile | Daily Log 2026-04-01
- Source: daily/2026-04-01.md
- Articles created: [[concepts/nextjs-project-structure]], [[concepts/tailwind-setup]]
- Articles updated: (none)

## [2026-04-02T09:00:00] query | "How do I handle auth redirects?"
- Consulted: [[concepts/supabase-auth]], [[concepts/nextjs-middleware]]
- Filed to: [[qa/auth-redirect-handling]]
```

---

## Article Formats

### Concept Articles (`knowledge/concepts/`)

One article per atomic piece of knowledge. These are facts, patterns, decisions, preferences, and lessons extracted from your conversations.

```markdown
---
title: "Concept Name"
aliases: [alternate-name, abbreviation]
tags: [domain, topic]
sources:
  - "daily/2026-04-01.md"
  - "daily/2026-04-03.md"
created: 2026-04-01
updated: 2026-04-03
---

# Concept Name

[2-4 sentence core explanation]

## Key Points

- [Bullet points, each self-contained]

## Details

[Deeper explanation, encyclopedia-style paragraphs]

## Related Concepts

- [[concepts/related-concept]] - How it connects

## Sources

- [[daily/2026-04-01.md]] - Initial discovery during project setup
- [[daily/2026-04-03.md]] - Updated after debugging session
```

### Connection Articles (`knowledge/connections/`)

Cross-cutting synthesis linking 2+ concepts. Created when a conversation reveals a non-obvious relationship.

```markdown
---
title: "Connection: X and Y"
connects:
  - "concepts/concept-x"
  - "concepts/concept-y"
sources:
  - "daily/2026-04-04.md"
created: 2026-04-04
updated: 2026-04-04
---

# Connection: X and Y

## The Connection

[What links these concepts]

## Key Insight

[The non-obvious relationship discovered]

## Evidence

[Specific examples from conversations]

## Related Concepts

- [[concepts/concept-x]]
- [[concepts/concept-y]]
```

### Q&A Articles (`knowledge/qa/`)

Filed answers from queries. Every complex question answered by the system can be permanently stored, making future queries smarter.

```markdown
---
title: "Q: Original Question"
question: "The exact question asked"
consulted:
  - "concepts/article-1"
  - "concepts/article-2"
filed: 2026-04-05
---

# Q: Original Question

## Answer

[The synthesized answer with [[wikilinks]] to sources]

## Sources Consulted

- [[concepts/article-1]] - Relevant because...
- [[concepts/article-2]] - Provided context on...

## Follow-Up Questions

- What about edge case X?
- How does this change if Y?
```

---

## Core Operations

### 1. Compile (daily/ -> knowledge/)

When processing a daily log:

1. Read the daily log file
2. Read `knowledge/index.md` to understand current knowledge state
3. Read existing articles that may need updating
4. For each piece of knowledge found in the log:
   - If an existing concept article covers this topic: UPDATE it with new information, add the daily log as a source
   - If it's a new topic: CREATE a new `concepts/` article
5. If the log reveals a non-obvious connection between 2+ existing concepts: CREATE a `connections/` article
6. UPDATE `knowledge/index.md` with new/modified entries
7. APPEND to `knowledge/log.md`

**Important guidelines:**
- A single daily log may touch 3-10 knowledge articles
- Prefer updating existing articles over creating near-duplicates
- Use Obsidian-style `[[wikilinks]]` with full relative paths from knowledge/
- Write in encyclopedia style - factual, concise, self-contained
- Every article must have YAML frontmatter
- Every article must link back to its source daily logs

### 2. Query (Ask the Knowledge Base)

1. Read `knowledge/index.md` (the master catalog)
2. Based on the question, identify 3-10 relevant articles from the index
3. Read those articles in full
4. Synthesize an answer with `[[wikilink]]` citations
5. If `--file-back` is specified: create a `knowledge/qa/` article and update index.md and log.md

**Why this works without RAG:** At personal knowledge base scale (50-500 articles), the LLM reading a structured index outperforms cosine similarity. The LLM understands what the question is really asking and selects pages accordingly. Embeddings find similar words; the LLM finds relevant concepts.

### 3. Lint (Health Checks)

Seven checks, run periodically:

1. **Broken links** - `[[wikilinks]]` pointing to non-existent articles
2. **Orphan pages** - Articles with zero inbound links from other articles
3. **Orphan sources** - Daily logs that haven't been compiled yet
4. **Stale articles** - Source daily log changed since article was last compiled
5. **Contradictions** - Conflicting claims across articles (requires LLM judgment)
6. **Missing backlinks** - A links to B but B doesn't link back to A
7. **Sparse articles** - Below 200 words, likely incomplete

Output: a markdown report with severity levels (error, warning, suggestion).

---

## Conventions

- **Wikilinks:** Use Obsidian-style `[[path/to/article]]` without `.md` extension

     5|Adapted from [Karpathy's LLM Knowledge Base](https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f) architecture, but instead of clipping web articles, the raw data is your own conversations with Claude Code.      8|

    18|3. The hooks activate automatically next time you open Claude Code
    19|
    20|From there, your conversations start accumulating. After 6 PM local time, the next session flush automatically triggers compilation of that day's logs into knowledge articles. You can also run `uv run python scripts/compile.py` manually at any time.

    23|

    25|Conversation -> SessionEnd/PreCompact hooks -> flush.py extracts knowledge

    31|- **flush.py** calls the Claude Agent SDK to decide what's worth saving, and after 6 PM triggers end-of-day compilation automatically
    32|- **compile.py** turns daily logs into organized concept articles with cross-references (triggered automatically or run manually)
    33|- **query.py** answers questions using index-guided retrieval (no RAG needed at personal scale)
    34|- **lint.py** runs 7 health checks (broken links, orphans, contradictions, staleness)

    39|uv run python scripts/compile.py                    # compile new daily logs
    40|uv run python scripts/query.py "question"            # ask the knowledge base
    41|uv run python scripts/query.py "question" --file-back # ask + save answer back
    42|uv run python scripts/lint.py                        # run health checks
    43|uv run python scripts/lint.py --structural-only      # free structural checks only
    44|```
    45|
    46|## Why No RAG?
    47|
    48|Karpathy's insight: at personal scale (50-500 articles), the LLM reading a structured `index.md` outperforms vector similarity.    228|## Core Operations
   229|
   230|### 1. Compile (daily/ -> knowledge/)
   231|
   232|When processing a daily log:
   233|
   234|1. Read the daily log file
   235|2. Read `knowledge/index.md` to understand current knowledge state
   236|3. Read existing articles that may need updating
   237|4. For each piece of knowledge found in the log:
   238|   - If an existing concept article covers this topic: UPDATE it with new information, add the daily log as a source
   239|   - If it's a new topic: CREATE a new `concepts/` article
   240|5. If the log reveals a non-obvious connection between 2+ existing concepts: CREATE a `connections/` article
   241|6. UPDATE `knowledge/index.md` with new/modified entries
   242|7. APPEND to `knowledge/log.md`
   243|
   244|**Important guidelines:**
   245|- A single daily log may touch 3-10 knowledge articles
   246|- Prefer updating existing articles over creating near-duplicates
   247|- Use Obsidian-style `[[wikilinks]]` with full relative paths from knowledge/
   248|- Write in encyclopedia style - factual, concise, self-contained
   249|- Every article must have YAML frontmatter
   250|- Every article must link back to its source daily logs
   251|
   252|### 2. Query (Ask the Knowledge Base)
   253|
   254|1. Read `knowledge/index.md` (the master catalog)
   255|2. Based on the question, identify 3-10 relevant articles from the index
   256|3. Read those articles in full
   257|4. Synthesize an answer with `[[wikilink]]` citations
   258|5. If `--file-back` is specified: create a `knowledge/qa/` article and update index.md and log.md
   259|
   260|**Why this works without RAG:** At personal knowledge base scale (50-500 articles), the LLM reading a structured index outperforms cosine similarity. The LLM understands what the question is really asking and selects pages accordingly. Embeddings find similar words; the LLM finds relevant concepts.
   261|
   262|### 3. Lint (Health Checks)
   263|
   264|Seven checks, run periodically:
   265|
   266|1. **Broken links** - `[[wikilinks]]` pointing to non-existent articles
   267|2. **Orphan pages** - Articles with zero inbound links from other articles
   268|3. **Orphan sources** - Daily logs that haven't been compiled yet
   269|4. **Stale articles** - Source daily log changed since article was last compiled
   270|5. **Contradictions** - Conflicting claims across articles (requires LLM judgment)
   271|6. **Missing backlinks** - A links to B but B doesn't link back to A
   272|7. **Sparse articles** - Below 200 words, likely incomplete
   273|
   274|Output: a markdown report with severity levels (error, warning, suggestion).
   275|
   276|---
   277|
   278|## Conventions
   279|
   280|- **Wikilinks:** Use Obsidian-style `[[path/to/article]]` without `.md` extension
   281|- **Writing style:** Encyclopedia-style, factual, third-person where appropriate
   282|- **Dates:** ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD for dates, full ISO for timestamps in log.md)
   283|- **File naming:** lowercase, hyphens for spaces (e.g., `supabase-row-level-security.md`)
   284|- **Frontmatter:** Every article must have YAML frontmatter with at minimum: title, sources, created, updated
   285|- **Sources:** Always link back to the daily log(s) that contributed to an article

   321|
   322|## Hook System (Automatic Capture)
   323|
   324|Hooks are configured in `.claude/settings.json` and fire automatically when you use Claude Code in this project.
   325|
   326|### `.claude/settings.json` Format
   327|
   328|```json
   329|{
   330|  "hooks": {
   331|    "SessionStart": [{ "matcher": "", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run python hooks/session-start.py", "timeout": 15 }] }],
   332|    "PreCompact": [{ "matcher": "", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run python hooks/pre-compact.py", "timeout": 10 }] }],
   333|    "SessionEnd": [{ "matcher": "", "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "uv run python hooks/session-end.py", "timeout": 10 }] }]
   334|  }
   335|}
   336|```
   337|
   338|Commands use simple relative paths from the project root. Empty `matcher` catches all events.
   339|
   340|### Hook Details
   341|
   342|**`session-start.py`** (SessionStart)
   343|- Pure local I/O, no API calls, runs in under 1 second
   344|- Reads `knowledge/index.md` and the most recent daily log
   345|- Outputs JSON to stdout: `{"hookSpecificOutput": {"hookEventName": "SessionStart", "additionalContext": "..."}}`
   346|- Claude sees the knowledge base index at the start of every session
   347|- Max context: 20,000 characters
   348|
   349|**`session-end.py`** (SessionEnd)
   350|- Reads hook input from stdin (JSON with `session_id`, `transcript_path`, `cwd`)
   351|- Copies the raw JSONL transcript to a temp file (no parsing in the hook - keeps it fast)
   352|- Spawns `flush.py` as a fully detached background process
   353|- Recursion guard: exits immediately if `CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY` env var is set
   354|
   355|**`pre-compact.py`** (PreCompact)
   356|- Same architecture as session-end.py
   357|- Fires before Claude Code auto-compacts the context window
   358|- Guards against empty `transcript_path` (known Claude Code bug #13668)
   359|- Critical for long sessions: captures context before summarization discards it
   360|
   361|**Why both PreCompact and SessionEnd?** Long-running sessions may trigger multiple auto-compactions before you close the session. Without PreCompact, intermediate context is lost to summarization before SessionEnd ever fires.
   362|
   363|### Background Flush Process (`flush.py`)
   364|
   365|Spawned by both hooks as a fully detached background process:
   366|- **Windows:** `CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP | DETACHED_PROCESS` flags
   367|- **Mac/Linux:** `start_new_session=True`
   368|
   369|This ensures flush.py survives after Claude Code's hook process exits.
   370|
   371|**What flush.py does:**
   372|1. Sets `CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY=memory_flush` env var (prevents recursive hook firing)
   373|2. Reads the pre-extracted conversation context from the temp `.md` file
   374|3. Skips if context is empty or if same session was flushed within 60 seconds (deduplication)
   375|4. Calls Claude Agent SDK (`query()` with `allowed_tools=[]`, `max_turns=2`)
   376|5. Claude decides what's worth saving - returns structured bullet points or `FLUSH_OK`
   377|6. Appends result to `daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md`
   378|7. Cleans up temp context file
   379|8. **End-of-day auto-compilation:** If it's past 6 PM local time (`COMPILE_AFTER_HOUR = 18`) and today's daily log has changed since its last compilation (hash comparison against `state.json`), spawns `compile.py` as another detached background process. This means compilation happens automatically once a day without needing a cron job or manual trigger.
   380|
   381|### JSONL Transcript Format
   382|
   383|Claude Code stores conversations as `.jsonl` files. Messages are nested under a `message` key:
   384|
   385|```python
   386|entry = json.loads(line)
   387|msg = entry.get("message", {})
   388|role = msg.get("role", "")     # "user" or "assistant"
   389|content = msg.get("content", "")  # string or list of content blocks
   390|```
   391|
   392|Content can be a string or a list of blocks (`{"type": "text", "text": "..."}` dicts).

   396|## Script Details
   397|
   398|### compile.py - The Compiler
   399|
   400|Uses the Claude Agent SDK's async streaming `query()`:
   401|
   402|```python
   403|async for message in query(
   404|    prompt=compile_prompt,
   405|    options=ClaudeAgentOptions(
   406|        cwd=str(ROOT_DIR),
   407|        system_prompt={"type": "preset", "preset": "claude_code"},
   408|        allowed_tools=["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Glob", "Grep"],
   409|        permission_mode="acceptEdits",
   410|        max_turns=30,
   411|    ),
   412|):
   413|```
   414|
   415|- Builds a prompt with: AGENTS.md schema, current index, all existing articles, and the daily log
   416|- Claude reads the daily log, decides what concepts to extract, and writes files directly
   417|- `permission_mode="acceptEdits"` auto-approves all file operations
   418|- Incremental: tracks SHA-256 hashes of daily logs in `state.json`, skips unchanged files
   419|- Cost: ~$0.45-0.65 per daily log (increases as KB grows)
   420|
   421|**CLI:**
   422|```bash
   423|uv run python scripts/compile.py              # compile new/changed only
   424|uv run python scripts/compile.py --all        # force recompile everything
   425|uv run python scripts/compile.py --file daily/2026-04-01.md
   426|uv run python scripts/compile.py --dry-run
   427|```
   428|
   429|### query.py - Index-Guided Retrieval
   430|
   431|Loads the entire knowledge base into context (index + all articles). No RAG.
   432|
   433|At personal KB scale (50-500 articles), the LLM reading a structured index outperforms vector similarity. The LLM understands what you're really asking; cosine similarity just finds similar words.
   434|
   435|**CLI:**
   436|```bash
   437|uv run python scripts/query.py "What auth patterns do I use?"
   438|uv run python scripts/query.py "What's my error handling strategy?" --file-back
   439|```
   440|
   441|With `--file-back`, creates a Q&A article in `knowledge/qa/` and updates the index and log. This is the compounding loop - every question makes the KB smarter.
   442|
   443|### lint.py - Health Checks
   444|
   445|Seven checks:
   446|
   447|| Check | Type | Catches |
   448||-------|------|---------|
   449|| Broken links | Structural | `[[wikilinks]]` to non-existent articles |
   450|| Orphan pages | Structural | Articles with zero inbound links |
   451|| Orphan sources | Structural | Daily logs not yet compiled |
   452|| Stale articles | Structural | Source logs changed since compilation |
   453|| Missing backlinks | Structural | A links to B but B doesn't link back |
   454|| Sparse articles | Structural | Under 200 words |
   455|| Contradictions | LLM | Conflicting claims across articles |
   456|
   457|**CLI:**
   458|```bash
   459|uv run python scripts/lint.py                    # all checks
   460|uv run python scripts/lint.py --structural-only  # skip LLM check (free)
   461|```
   462|
   463|Reports saved to `reports/lint-YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

   506|## Customization
   507|
   508|### Additional Article Types
   509|
   510|Add directories like `people/`, `projects/`, `tools/` to `knowledge/`. Define the article format in this file (AGENTS.md) and update `utils.py`'s `list_wiki_articles()` to include them.
   511|
   512|### Obsidian Integration
   513|
   514|The knowledge base is pure markdown with `[[wikilinks]]` - works natively in Obsidian. Point a vault at `knowledge/` for graph view, backlinks, and search.
   515|
   516|### Scaling Beyond Index-Guided Retrieval
   517|
   518|At ~2,000+ articles / ~2M+ tokens, the index becomes too large for the context window. At that point, add hybrid RAG (keyword + semantic search) as a retrieval layer before the LLM. See Karpathy's recommendation of `qmd` by Tobi Lutke for search at scale.

    35|async def compile_daily_log(log_path: Path, state: dict) -> float:

    73|

{"content": "     1|"""
     2|Memory flush agent - extracts important knowledge from conversation context.
     3|
     4|Spawned by session-end.py or pre-compact.py as a background process. Reads
     5|pre-extracted conversation context from a .md file, uses the Claude Agent SDK
     6|to decide what's worth saving, and appends the result to today's daily log.
     7|
     8|Usage:
     9|    uv run python flush.py <context_file.md> <session_id>
    10|"""
    11|
    12|from __future__ import annotations
    13|
    14|# Recursion prevention: set this BEFORE any imports that might trigger Claude
    15|import os
    16|os.environ["CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY"] = "memory_flush"
    17|
    18|import asyncio
    19|import json
    20|import logging
    21|import sys
    22|import time
    23|from datetime import datetime, timezone
    24|from pathlib import Path
    25|
    26|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    27|DAILY_DIR = ROOT / "daily"
    28|SCRIPTS_DIR = ROOT / "scripts"
    29|STATE_FILE = SCRIPTS_DIR / "last-flush.json"
    30|LOG_FILE = SCRIPTS_DIR / "flush.log"
    31|
    32|# Set up file-based logging so we can verify the background process ran.
    33|# The parent process sends stdout/stderr to DEVNULL (to avoid the inherited
    34|# file handle bug on Windows), so this is our only observability channel.
    35|logging.basicConfig(
    36|    filename=str(LOG_FILE),
    37|    level=logging.INFO,
    38|    format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    39|    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    40|)
    41|
    42|
    43|def load_flush_state() -> dict:
    44|    if STATE_FILE.exists():
    45|        try:
    46|            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    47|        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
    48|            pass
    49|    return {}
    50|
    51|
    52|def save_flush_state(state: dict) -> None:
    53|    STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state), encoding="utf-8")
    54|
    55|
    56|def append_to_daily_log(content: str, section: str = "Session") -> None:
    57|    """Append content to today's daily log."""
    58|    today = datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone()
    59|    log_path = DAILY_DIR / f"{today.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}.md"
    60|
    61|    if not log_path.exists():
    62|        DAILY_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    63|        log_path.write_text(
    64|            f"# Daily Log: {today.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}\n\n## Sessions\n\n## Memory Maintenance\n\n",
    65|            encoding="utf-8",
    66|        )
    67|
    68|    time_str = today.strftime("%H:%M")
    69|    entry = f"### {section} ({time_str})\n\n{content}\n\n"
    70|
    71|    with open(log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    72|        f.write(entry)
    73|
    74|
    75|async def run_flush(context: str) -> str:
    76|    """Use Claude Agent SDK to extract important knowledge from conversation context."""
    77|    from claude_agent_sdk import (
    78|        AssistantMessage,
    79|        ClaudeAgentOptions,
    80|        ResultMessage,
    81|        TextBlock,
    82|        query,
    83|    )
    84|
    85|    prompt = f"""Review the conversation context below and respond with a concise summary
    86|of important items that should be preserved in the daily log.
    87|Do NOT use any tools — just return plain text.
    88|
    89|Format your response as a structured daily log entry with these sections:
    90|
    91|**Context:** [One line about what the user was working on]
    92|
    93|**Key Exchanges:**
    94|- [Important Q&A or discussions]
    95|
    96|**Decisions Made:**
    97|- [Any decisions with rationale]
    98|
    99|**Lessons Learned:**
   100|- [Gotchas, patterns, or insights discovered]
   101|
   102|**Action Items:**
   103|- [Follow-ups or TODOs mentioned]
   104|
   105|Skip anything that is:
   106|- Routine tool calls or file reads
   107|- Content that's trivial or obvious
   108|- Trivial back-and-forth or clarification exchanges
   109|
   110|Only include sections that have actual content. If nothing is worth saving,
   111|respond with exactly: FLUSH_OK
   112|
   113|## Conversation Context
   114|
   115|{context}"""
   116|
   117|    response = ""
   118|
   119|    try:
   120|        async for message in query(
   121|            prompt=prompt,
   122|            options=ClaudeAgentOptions(
   123|                cwd=str(ROOT),
   124|                allowed_tools=[],
   125|                max_turns=2,
   126|            ),
   127|        ):
   128|            if isinstance(message, AssistantMessage):
   129|                for block in message.content:
   130|                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
   131|                        response += block.text
   132|            elif isinstance(message, ResultMessage):
   133|                pass
   134|    except Exception as e:
   135|        import traceback
   136|        logging.error("Agent SDK error: %s\n%s", e, traceback.format_exc())
   137|        response = f"FLUSH_ERROR: {type(e).__name__}: {e}"
   138|
   139|    return response
   140|
   141|
   142|COMPILE_AFTER_HOUR = 18  # 6 PM local time
   143|
   144|
   145|def maybe_trigger_compilation() -> None:
   146|    """If it's past the compile hour and today's log hasn't been compiled, run compile.py."""
   147|    import subprocess as _sp
   148|
   149|    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone()
   150|    if now.hour < COMPILE_AFTER_HOUR:
   151|        return
   152|
   153|    # Check if today's log has already been compiled
   154|    today_log = f"{now.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}.md"
   155|    compile_state_file = SCRIPTS_DIR / "state.json"
   156|    if compile_state_file.exists():
   157|        try:
   158|            compile_state = json.loads(compile_state_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   159|            ingested = compile_state.get("ingested", {})
   160|            if today_log in ingested:
   161|                # Already compiled today - check if the log has changed since
   162|                from hashlib import sha256
   163|                log_path = DAILY_DIR / today_log
   164|                if log_path.exists():
   165|                    current_hash = sha256(log_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()[:16]
   166|                    if ingested[today_log].get("hash") == current_hash:
   167|                        return  # log unchanged since last compile
   168|        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
   169|            pass
   170|
   171|    compile_script = SCRIPTS_DIR / "compile.py"
   172|    if not compile_script.exists():
   173|        return
   174|
   175|    logging.info("End-of-day compilation triggered (after %d:00)", COMPILE_AFTER_HOUR)
   176|
   177|    cmd = ["uv", "run", "--directory", str(ROOT), "python", str(compile_script)]
   178|
   179|    kwargs: dict = {}
   180|    if sys.platform == "win32":
   181|        kwargs["creationflags"] = _sp.CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP | _sp.DETACHED_PROCESS
   182|    else:
   183|        kwargs["start_new_session"] = True
   184|
   185|    try:
   186|        log_handle = open(str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "compile.log"), "a")
   187|        _sp.Popen(cmd, stdout=log_handle, stderr=_sp.STDOUT, cwd=str(ROOT), **kwargs)
   188|    except Exception as e:
   189|        logging.error("Failed to spawn compile.py: %s", e)
   190|
   191|
   192|def main():
   193|    if len(sys.argv) < 3:
   194|        logging.error("Usage: %s <context_file.md> <session_id>", sys.argv[0])
   195|        sys.exit(1)
   196|
   197|    context_file = Path(sys.argv[1])
   198|    session_id = sys.argv[2]
   199|
   200|    logging.info("flush.py started for session %s, context: %s", session_id, context_file)
   201|
   202|    if not context_file.exists():
   203|        logging.error("Context file not found: %s", context_file)
   204|        return
   205|
   206|    # Deduplication: skip if same session was flushed within 60 seconds
   207|    state = load_flush_state()
   208|    if (
   209|        state.get("session_id") == session_id
   210|        and time.time() - state.get("timestamp", 0) < 60
   211|    ):
   212|        logging.info("Skipping duplicate flush for session %s", session_id)
   213|        context_file.unlink(missing_ok=True)
   214|        return
   215|
   216|    # Read pre-extracted context
   217|    context = context_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
   218|    if not context:
   219|        logging.info("Context file is empty, skipping")
   220|        context_file.unlink(missing_ok=True)
   221|        return
   222|
   223|    logging.info("Flushing session %s: %d chars", session_id, len(context))
   224|
   225|    # Run the LLM extraction
   226|    response = asyncio.run(run_flush(context))
   227|
   228|    # Append to daily log
   229|    if "FLUSH_OK" in response:
   230|        logging.info("Result: FLUSH_OK")
   231|        append_to_daily_log(
   232|            "FLUSH_OK - Nothing worth saving from this session", "Memory Flush"
   233|        )
   234|    elif "FLUSH_ERROR" in response:
   235|        logging.error("Result: %s", response)
   236|        append_to_daily_log(response, "Memory Flush")
   237|    else:
   238|        logging.info("Result: saved to daily log (%d chars)", len(response))
   239|        append_to_daily_log(response, "Session")
   240|
   241|    # Update dedup state
   242|    save_flush_state({"session_id": session_id, "timestamp": time.time()})
   243|
   244|    # Clean up context file
   245|    context_file.unlink(missing_ok=True)
   246|
   247|    # End-of-day auto-compilation: if it's past the compile hour and today's
   248|    # log hasn't been compiled yet, trigger compile.py in the background.
   249|    maybe_trigger_compilation()
   250|
   251|    logging.info("Flush complete for session %s", session_id)
   252|
   253|
   254|if __name__ == "__main__":
   255|    main()

     2|Lint the knowledge base for structural and semantic health.
     3|
     4|Runs 7 checks: broken links, orphan pages, orphan sources, stale articles,
     5|contradictions (LLM), missing backlinks, and sparse articles.
     6|
     7|Usage:

    10|"""
    11|
    12|from __future__ import annotations
    13|
    14|import argparse
    15|import asyncio
    16|from pathlib import Path
    17|
    18|from config import KNOWLEDGE_DIR, REPORTS_DIR, now_iso, today_iso
    19|from utils import (
    20|    count_inbound_links,
    21|    extract_wikilinks,

    23|    get_article_word_count,
    24|    list_raw_files,
    25|    list_wiki_articles,
    26|    load_state,
    27|    read_all_wiki_content,

    31|
    32|ROOT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    33|
    34|
    35|def check_broken_links() -> list[dict]:
    36|    """Check for [[wikilinks]] that point to non-existent articles."""
    37|    issues = []
    38|    for article in list_wiki_articles():
    39|        content = article.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    40|        rel = article.relative_to(KNOWLEDGE_DIR)
    41|        for link in extract_wikilinks(content):
    42|            if link.startswith("daily/"):
    43|                continue  # daily log references are valid
    44|            if not wiki_article_exists(link):
    45|                issues.append({
    46|                    "severity": "error",
    47|                    "check": "broken_link",
    48|                    "file": str(rel),
    49|                    "detail": f"Broken link: [[{link}]] - target does not exist",
    50|                })
    51|    return issues
    52|
    53|
    54|def check_orphan_pages() -> list[dict]:
    55|    """Check for articles with zero inbound links."""
    56|    issues = []
    57|    for article in list_wiki_articles():
    58|        rel = article.relative_to(KNOWLEDGE_DIR)
    59|        link_target = str(rel).replace(".md", "").replace("\\", "/")
    60|        inbound = count_inbound_links(link_target)
    61|        if inbound == 0:
    62|            issues.append({
    63|                "severity": "warning",
    64|                "check": "orphan_page",
    65|                "file": str(rel),
    66|                "detail": f"Orphan page: no other articles link to [[{link_target}]]",
    67|            })
    68|    return issues
    69|
    70|
    71|def check_orphan_sources() -> list[dict]:
    72|    """Check for daily logs that haven't been compiled yet."""
    73|    state = load_state()
    74|    ingested = state.get("ingested", {})
    75|    issues = []
    76|    for log_path in list_raw_files():

    82|                "detail": f"Uncompiled daily log: {log_path.name} has not been ingested",
    83|            })
    84|    return issues

    86|
    87|def check_stale_articles() -> list[dict]:
    88|    """Check if source daily logs have changed since compilation."""

    90|    ingested = state.get("ingested", {})
    91|    issues = []
    92|    for log_path in list_raw_files():

    94|        if rel in ingested:

    96|            current_hash = file_hash(log_path)

   103|                })
   104|    return issues

   106|
   107|def check_missing_backlinks() -> list[dict]:
   108|    """Check for asymmetric links: A links to B but B doesn't link to A."""
   109|    issues = []
   110|    for article in list_wiki_articles():

   112|        rel = article.relative_to(KNOWLEDGE_DIR)

   115|        for link in extract_wikilinks(content):

   119|            if target_path.exists():
   120|                target_content = target_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
   121|                if f"[[{source_link}]]" not in target_content:
   122|                    issues.append({
   123|                        "severity": "suggestion",

   126|                        "detail": f"[[{source_link}]] links to [[{link}]] but not vice versa",
   127|                        "auto_fixable": True,
   128|                    })
   129|    return issues
   130|
   131|
   132|def check_sparse_articles() -> list[dict]:
   133|    """Check for articles with fewer than 200 words."""
   134|    issues = []
   135|    for article in list_wiki_articles():
   136|        word_count = get_article_word_count(article)
   137|        if word_count < 200:
   138|            rel = article.relative_to(KNOWLEDGE_DIR)
   139|            issues.append({
   140|                "severity": "suggestion",

     2|SessionEnd hook - captures conversation transcript for memory extraction.
     3|
     4|When a Claude Code session ends, this hook reads the transcript path from
     5|stdin, extracts conversation context, and spawns flush.py as a background
     6|process to extract knowledge into the daily log.
     7|
     8|The hook itself does NO API calls - only local file I/O for speed (<10s).
     9|"""
    10|
    11|from __future__ import annotations
    12|
    13|import json
    14|import logging
    15|import os
    16|import re
    17|import subprocess
    18|import sys
    19|from datetime import datetime, timezone
    20|from pathlib import Path
    21|
    22|# Recursion guard: if we were spawned by flush.py (which calls Agent SDK,
    23|# which runs Claude Code, which would fire this hook again), exit immediately.
    24|if os.environ.get("CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY"):
    25|    sys.exit(0)
    26|
    27|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
    28|DAILY_DIR = ROOT / "daily"
    29|SCRIPTS_DIR = ROOT / "scripts"
    30|STATE_DIR = SCRIPTS_DIR
    31|
    32|logging.basicConfig(
    33|    filename=str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "flush.log"),
    34|    level=logging.INFO,
    35|    format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s [hook] %(message)s",
    36|    datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
    37|)
    38|
    39|MAX_TURNS = 30
    40|MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS = 15_000
    41|MIN_TURNS_TO_FLUSH = 1
    42|
    43|
    44|def extract_conversation_context(transcript_path: Path) -> tuple[str, int]:
    45|    """Read JSONL transcript and extract last ~N conversation turns as markdown."""
    46|    turns: list[str] = []
    47|
    48|    with open(transcript_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
    49|        for line in f:
    50|            line = line.strip()
    51|            if not line:
    52|                continue
    53|            try:
    54|                entry = json.loads(line)
    55|            except json.JSONDecodeError:
    56|                continue
    57|
    58|            msg = entry.get("message", {})
    59|            if isinstance(msg, dict):
    60|                role = msg.get("role", "")
    61|                content = msg.get("content", "")
    62|            else:
    63|                role = entry.get("role", "")
    64|                content = entry.get("content", "")
    65|
    66|            if role not in ("user", "assistant"):
    67|                continue
    68|
    69|            if isinstance(content, list):
    70|                text_parts = []
    71|                for block in content:
    72|                    if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
    73|                        text_parts.append(block.get("text", ""))
    74|                    elif isinstance(block, str):
    75|                        text_parts.append(block)
    76|                content = "\n".join(text_parts)
    77|
    78|            if isinstance(content, str) and content.strip():
    79|                label = "User" if role == "user" else "Assistant"
    80|                turns.append(f"**{label}:** {content.strip()}\n")
    81|
    82|    recent = turns[-MAX_TURNS:]
    83|    context = "\n".join(recent)
    84|
    85|    if len(context) > MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS:
    86|        context = context[-MAX_CONTEXT_CHARS:]
    87|        boundary = context.find("\n**")
    88|        if boundary > 0:
    89|            context = context[boundary + 1 :]
    90|
    91|    return context, len(recent)
    92|
    93|
    94|def main() -> None:
    95|    # Read hook input from stdin
    96|    # Claude Code on Windows may pass paths with unescaped backslashes
    97|    try:
    98|        raw_input = sys.stdin.read()
    99|        try:
   100|            hook_input: dict = json.loads(raw_input)
   101|        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   102|            fixed_input = re.sub(r'(?<!\\)\\(?!["\\])', r'\\\\', raw_input)
   103|            hook_input = json.loads(fixed_input)
   104|    except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError, EOFError) as e:
   105|        logging.error("Failed to parse stdin: %s", e)
   106|        return
   107|
   108|    session_id = hook_input.get("session_id", "unknown")
   109|    source = hook_input.get("source", "unknown")
   110|    transcript_path_str = hook_input.get("transcript_path", "")
   111|
   112|    logging.info("SessionEnd fired: session=%s source=%s", session_id, source)
   113|
   114|    if not transcript_path_str or not isinstance(transcript_path_str, str):
   115|        logging.info("SKIP: no transcript path")
   116|        return
   117|
   118|    transcript_path = Path(transcript_path_str)
   119|    if not transcript_path.exists():
   120|        logging.info("SKIP: transcript missing: %s", transcript_path_str)
   121|        return
   122|
   123|    # Extract conversation context in the hook (fast, no API calls)
   124|    try:
   125|        context, turn_count = extract_conversation_context(transcript_path)
   126|    except Exception as e:
   127|        logging.error("Context extraction failed: %s", e)
   128|        return
   129|
   130|    if not context.strip():
   131|        logging.info("SKIP: empty context")
   132|        return
   133|
   134|    if turn_count < MIN_TURNS_TO_FLUSH:
   135|        logging.info("SKIP: only %d turns (min %d)", turn_count, MIN_TURNS_TO_FLUSH)
   136|        return
   137|
   138|    # Write context to a temp file for the background process
   139|    timestamp = datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone().strftime("%Y%m%d-%H%M%S")
   140|    context_file = STATE_DIR / f"session-flush-{session_id}-{timestamp}.md"
   141|    context_file.write_text(context, encoding="utf-8")
   142|
   143|    # Spawn flush.py as a background process
   144|    flush_script = SCRIPTS_DIR / "flush.py"
   145|
   146|    cmd = [
   147|        "uv",
   148|        "run",
   149|        "--directory",
   150|        str(ROOT),
   151|        "python",
   152|        str(flush_script),
   153|        str(context_file),

   157|    # On Windows, use CREATE_NO_WINDOW to avoid flash console window.
   158|    # Do NOT use DETACHED_PROCESS — it breaks the Agent SDK's subprocess I/O.
   159|    creation_flags = subprocess.CREATE_NO_WINDOW if sys.platform == "win32" else 0
   160|

COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS: claude-memory-compiler

WHAT IT IS:
A personal knowledge base system by Cole Medin (coleam00) that automatically compiles knowledge from your Claude Code conversations into a structured, searchable wiki. It's directly adapted from Andrej Karpathy's "LLM Knowledge Base" gist, but with a key twist: instead of clipping web articles, the raw data source is your own AI coding conversations.

THE KARPATHY CONNECTION:
Karpathy proposed an architecture where an LLM acts as a "compiler" that takes raw information and produces structured, queryable knowledge. His key insight was that at personal scale (50-500 articles), having an LLM read a structured index outperforms vector similarity / RAG. The LLM understands what you're really asking; cosine similarity just finds similar words. This project implements that idea end-to-end, using the specific compiler analogy:
- daily/ = source code (raw conversations)
- LLM = compiler (extracts and organizes)
- knowledge/ = executable (structured wiki)
- lint = test suite (health checks)
- queries = runtime (using the knowledge)

ARCHITECTURE (3 Layers):

Layer 1 - daily/ (Immutable Source): Append-only daily log files (YYYY-MM-DD.md) capturing session summaries with context, key exchanges, decisions, lessons learned, and action items.

Layer 2 - knowledge/ (LLM-Owned Output): Structured wiki with:
- index.md: Master catalog table (THE retrieval mechanism)
- log.md: Append-only build log
- concepts/: Atomic knowledge articles with YAML frontmatter
- connections/: Cross-cutting insights linking 2+ concepts
- qa/: Filed query answers that compound knowledge over time

Layer 3 - AGENTS.md (Schema/Spec): The "compiler specification" that tells the LLM how to format, compile, and maintain the knowledge base.

KEY FILES AND WHAT THEY DO:

hooks/session-start.py - Fires at every Claude Code session start. Reads knowledge/index.md + recent daily log and injects them as context via JSON stdout (hookSpecificOutput). Pure local I/O, no API calls, <1 second. Max 20K chars.

hooks/session-end.py - Fires when a session ends. Reads the JSONL transcript from stdin, extracts last ~30 conversation turns as markdown, writes to a temp file, and spawns flush.py as a detached background process. Has a recursion guard (CLAUDE_INVOKED_BY env var) to prevent infinite loops when flush.py itself calls Claude.

hooks/pre-compact.py - Same as session-end but fires BEFORE Claude Code auto-compacts (summarizes) the context window. Critical for long sessions where intermediate context would be lost to summarization before SessionEnd fires. Has a higher MIN_TURNS_TO_FLUSH (5 vs 1).

scripts/flush.py - The background memory extraction agent. Uses Claude Agent SDK (query() with allowed_tools=[], max_turns=2) to decide what's worth saving from a conversation. Outputs structured bullet points or "FLUSH_OK" if nothing is noteworthy. Appends results to daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Has deduplication (skips same session within 60 seconds). After 6 PM local time, auto-triggers compile.py if today's log has changed (hash comparison via state.json). Cost: ~$0.02-0.05 per flush.

scripts/compile.py - The "compiler" itself. Uses Claude Agent SDK with streaming async query(), giving the LLM tools (Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep) and permission_mode="acceptEdits" so it can directly create/modify files. Feeds it the full AGENTS.md schema, current index, all existing articles, and the daily log. The LLM then writes concept articles, connection articles, updates index.md and log.md. Tracks SHA-256 hashes in state.json for incremental compilation. Cost: ~$0.45-0.65 per daily log.

scripts/query.py - Index-guided retrieval with NO RAG. Loads the entire knowledge base (index + all articles) into context. The LLM reads the index, identifies 3-10 relevant articles, reads them in full, and synthesizes an answer with [[wikilink]] citations. With --file-back, creates a Q&A article that compounds the KB. Cost: ~$0.15-0.40 per query.

scripts/lint.py - 7 health checks: broken wikilinks, orphan pages (no inbound links), orphan sources (uncompiled daily logs), stale articles (source changed since compilation), missing backlinks (asymmetric links), sparse articles (<200 words), and contradictions (LLM-powered, the only non-free check). Generates markdown reports in reports/.

scripts/config.py - Path constants (ROOT_DIR, DAILY_DIR, KNOWLEDGE_DIR, etc.) and timestamp helpers.

scripts/utils.py - Shared utilities: state management (state.json), file hashing (SHA-256), slugification, wikilink extraction via regex, wiki content reading, article listing, inbound link counting, word counting (excluding YAML frontmatter).

CLEVER IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- No API key needed - uses Claude Code's built-in credentials at ~/.claude/.credentials.json via the Claude Agent SDK
- Personal use of Claude Agent SDK is covered under existing Claude subscriptions (Max/Team/Enterprise) - no separate API billing
- Cross-platform: Windows uses CREATE_NO_WINDOW / CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP | DETACHED_PROCESS; Mac/Linux uses start_new_session=True for background processes
- Handles Windows path backslash escaping issues in JSON parsing
- Guards against Claude Code bug #13668 (empty transcript_path in PreCompact)
- End-of-day compilation is automatic (after 6 PM) without cron jobs - triggered opportunistically by flush.py
- Obsidian-compatible: pure markdown with [[wikilinks]], works natively for graph view and backlinks
- Scaling path: at ~2000+ articles when index exceeds context window, the README recommends adding hybrid RAG (referencing Karpathy's recommendation of qmd by Tobi Lutke)
